frameworks.name: manifold capacity analysis (mean-field theoretic)
frameworks.one_line: neural population responses as class manifolds whose linear separability is quantified.

frameworks.name: category learning / perceptual learning
frameworks.one_line: behavioral improvement framed via feedback-driven categorization.

core_question: How does the geometry of V4 and IT population activity reshape as a monkey learns to categorize visual stimuli?
key_assumptions: population responses to stimuli in a category form a manifold-like representation
manifold capacity indexes category separability relevant to behavior
chronic recordings track the same population across learning days
manipulations: training day across the course of learning a category boundary
stimulus attributes varied along two independent dimensions
key_findings: manifold capacity for the two category classes increased in both V4 and IT as learning progressed
representations of the two categories became more linearly separable over training
interpretations: behavioral improvement during category learning is supported by geometric reshaping of ventral-stream population codes toward greater class separability
